questions.stem: Which PowerShell Event ID captures the full content of executed script blocks?

answers.feedback: Event ID 4624 is a successful logon event, not script block logging.
Correct. Event ID 4104 in the PowerShell/Operational log captures script block content when Script Block Logging is enabled.
Event ID 4688 logs process creation, not PowerShell script block content.
Event ID 7045 logs new service installation, not PowerShell activity.

questions.explanation: Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) captures the full text of all PowerShell script blocks to the Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational log.

questions.stem: Which PowerShell cmdlet retrieves Windows Security event log entries filtered by Event ID?

answers.feedback: Correct. Get-WinEvent with a filter hashtable is the recommended approach for querying specific event IDs efficiently.
Get-EventLog can filter by event ID via -InstanceId, but Get-WinEvent with a filter hashtable is more efficient and is not deprecated in PS 5.1.
Search-EventLog is not a valid PowerShell cmdlet.
Read-EventLog is not a valid PowerShell cmdlet.

questions.explanation: Get-WinEvent with -FilterHashtable is the preferred method in PowerShell 5.1 for querying event logs by ID. It is significantly faster than Get-EventLog on large logs because filtering happens at the provider level.

questions.stem: Under the current TSA pipeline security directive, what is the minimum retention period for cybersecurity event logs on pipeline OT systems?

answers.feedback: 30 days is insufficient. The current TSA pipeline security directive requires a longer retention period to support incident investigations.
90 days is a common IT benchmark but does not meet the current TSA pipeline security directive requirement for pipeline operators.
Correct. The current TSA pipeline security directive requires pipeline operators to retain cybersecurity event logs for a minimum of 12 months to support investigations and compliance audits.
7 years is a financial records standard (SOX), not a TSA cybersecurity log retention requirement.

questions.explanation: The current TSA pipeline security directive mandates a 12-month minimum retention period for cybersecurity event logs. Operators should store logs in a tamper-resistant location separate from the OT network to ensure they are available for post-incident analysis.
